articles.author: Ben Finley, Matthew Lee, Farnoush Amiri
articles.description: In 24 hours, the Trump administration’s stance on the Iran war has pinballed from declarations that a tenuous ceasefire was holding and military operations were over to new threats of bombing the Islamic Republic. The administration's shifting and often contradictory messaging throughout the Iran war has produced ever more confusion this week. That's because President Donald Trump and his aides have presented a dizzying narrative about the U.S. strategy to unblock the Strait of Hormuz and wrap up the war, which has drastically changed over the course of mere hours. The closure of the vital oil shipping corridor has driven up fuel prices and put political pressure on the administration.
articles.title: Trump messaging about next steps in Iran creates confusion

articles.author: E. Eduardo Castillo, Elena Becatoros
articles.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is threatening Iran with more bombing if it doesn’t reopen strait after report that agreement is emerging to end war. Trump posted on social media Wednesday that the war with Iran could soon end and oil and natural gas shipments could restart. But that all depends on Iran accepting a reported agreement that the U.S. president did not detail. Trump wrote: “If they don’t agree, the bombing starts."
articles.title: Trump threatens Iran with bombing if it doesn’t reopen Strait of Hormuz

articles.author: Thomas Adamson
articles.description: France’s aircraft carrier strike group is moving south of the Suez Canal into the Red Sea. French President Emmanuel Macron says this is in preparation for a potential French-British mission in the Strait of Hormuz. The deployment brings Europe’s most powerful warship closer to the strait, which has been effectively closed due to the war in Iran. This has stranded hundreds of ships and caused a major disruption in the global oil market. Macron says the mission aims to restore maritime security and confidence among shipowners and insurers. The effort is distinct from the U.S. “Project Freedom” that launched and then paused this week.
articles.title: French carrier group moves closer to Strait of Hormuz for possible mission

articles.author: Bassem Mroue
articles.description: Pope Leo XIV has surprised 13 priests from southern Lebanon with a video call from Rome. He told them they are in his prayers and that he hoped for peace along the tense border with Israel. The priests, from villages like Rmeish and Marjayoun, were attending an online meeting with the Vatican’s ambassador to Lebanon when the pope joined. Father Najib al-Amil told The Associated Press the pope’s words were reassuring as they live in constant worry. The call comes amid ongoing tensions despite a ceasefire. Recently, the Israeli military demolished a Catholic convent, raising concerns among the Christian community.
articles.title: Pope Leo XIV comforts 13 priests from southern Lebanon with a surprise video call

articles.author: Isabel DeBre
articles.description: Officials and experts in Argentina are scrambling to figure out if their country is the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak that has gripped an Atlantic cruise. Argentina is consistently ranked by the World Health Organization as having the highest incidence of the rare, rat-borne disease in Latin America. And it's seeing a surge of hantavirus cases that many local public health researchers attribute to the accelerating effects of climate change. The Argentine Health Ministry on Tuesday reported 101 hantavirus infections since June 2025. That's roughly double the caseload recorded over the same period the previous year.
articles.title: Argentina investigates hantavirus outbreak on Atlantic cruise

articles.author: Annie Risemberg, Misper Apawu, Aleksandar Furtula, Isabel DeBre
articles.description: The U.N. health agency says two patients with hantavirus and one suspected of infection were evacuated from a cruise ship at the center of a deadly outbreak. Two of the patients arrived at Amsterdam’s airport Wednesday evening and were driven off in ambulances. The ship departed from Cape Verde and headed to Spain's Canary Islands. The World Health Organization says eight cases have been recorded, with five confirmed by lab tests. Three people have died. Two Argentine officials investigating the origins of the outbreak say the government’s leading hypothesis is that a Dutch couple contracted the virus during a bird-watching outing before boarding.
articles.title: 3 patients are evacuated from cruise ship with hantavirus outbreak

articles.author: Gerald Imray
articles.description: A deadly outbreak of the rare hantavirus unfolded over the course of weeks on a cruise ship that sailed from Argentina toward Antarctica and then across the Atlantic Ocean. Information from the cruise operator, health authorities and ship tracking data shows nearly a month passed between when the first passenger fell sick and died in the South Atlantic and tests in South Africa that first confirmed hantavirus infections. Three passengers have died. One is in intensive care in a South African hospital. Three others were evacuated from the ship Wednesday. Another man who left the ship earlier in the voyage tested positive in Switzerland.
articles.title: How a deadly hantavirus outbreak unfolded on a cruise ship

articles.description: iulia Gallo, Postdoctoral Scientist in the Viral Glycoproteins Group at The Pirbright Institute told The Associated Press that "the most important thing is not to create panic" as the transmission rate for the virus is very low.
articles.title: Virologist on the hantavirus outbreak on board a cruise ship

articles.description: A runner in a grueling 250-mile ultramarathon on trails across northern Arizona has died after experiencing a medical emergency. Race organizers and local law enforcement confirmed the death Wednesday. Local first responders attended to a woman in her 40s who was participating in the race and collapsed at a trailhead near Prescott. Cocodona 250 ultramarathon codirector Erika Snyder declined to provide further details at the request of the runner's relatives. Ultramarathons can test the boundaries of human endurance, but deaths are relatively rare in the increasingly popular sport. The winner of the Cocodona 250 finished to a roaring crowd in Flagstaff.
articles.title: Runner who had a medical emergency dies in Arizona ultramarathon

articles.author: Michael R. Sisak, Larry Neumeister
articles.description: A note Jeffrey Epstein’s former cellmate claimed he found after the financier’s first jail suicide attempt has been made public after it had been sealed and locked in a courthouse vault for nearly five years as part of an unrelated legal dispute. A judge released the note Wednesday after The New York Times petitioned last week to unseal it and other documents in a case involving the cellmate, Nicholas Tartaglione. Among other things, the note contains the phrase “time to say goodbye." Tartaglione is a former police officer who is serving a life sentence for killing four people. It isn't clear who wrote the note Tartaglione claimed he found. It wasn’t mentioned in the lengthy government reports examining the circumstances of Epstein’s death.
articles.title: Judge releases note reportedly found after Epstein's suspected suicide try
